subject: Life Skills
title: Active Citizenship
tagline: Your rights and responsibilities as a South African citizen!

stages.name: Stage 1
stages.question: What is a 'right'?
options: The Constitution
A responsibility citizens have to their community
Taking part in improving your community
Something everyone is entitled to
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Something everyone is entitled to is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Something everyone is entitled to.

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.question: What is a 'responsibility'?
options: The Constitution
Obeying the law
A responsibility citizens have to their community
A duty you are expected to carry out
stages.correctOption: A duty you are expected to carry out
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A duty you are expected to carry out is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A duty you are expected to carry out.

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.question: What is active citizenship?
options: A responsibility citizens have to their community
A duty you are expected to carry out
Taking part in improving your community
Something everyone is entitled to
stages.correctOption: Taking part in improving your community
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Taking part in improving your community is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Taking part in improving your community.

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.question: What document protects South Africans' rights?
options: Taking part in improving your community
Giving your time to help others without pay
Obeying the law
The Constitution
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The Constitution is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The Constitution.

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.question: What is one responsibility of a citizen?
options: The South African Human Rights Commission
It lets citizens choose their leaders
Taking part in improving your community
Obeying the law
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Obeying the law is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Obeying the law.

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.question: Why is voting an important right?
options: Taking part in improving your community
Something everyone is entitled to
It lets citizens choose their leaders
The South African Human Rights Commission
stages.correctOption: It lets citizens choose their leaders
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! It lets citizens choose their leaders is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is It lets citizens choose their leaders.

stages.name: Stage 7
stages.question: What is 'civic duty'?
options: Obeying the law
A responsibility citizens have to their community
Giving your time to help others without pay
The Constitution
stages.correctOption: A responsibility citizens have to their community
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A responsibility citizens have to their community is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A responsibility citizens have to their community.

stages.name: Stage 8
stages.question: What body protects human rights in South Africa?
options: The South African Human Rights Commission
Taking part in improving your community
Obeying the law
A responsibility citizens have to their community
stages.correctOption: The South African Human Rights Commission
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The South African Human Rights Commission is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The South African Human Rights Commission.

stages.name: Stage 9
stages.question: Why is freedom of speech important?
options: Obeying the law
It allows people to share their views safely
A responsibility citizens have to their community
Giving your time to help others without pay
stages.correctOption: It allows people to share their views safely
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! It allows people to share their views safely is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is It allows people to share their views safely.

stages.name: Stage 10
stages.question: What is 'volunteering'?
options: The Constitution
Giving your time to help others without pay
It allows people to share their views safely
Something everyone is entitled to
stages.correctOption: Giving your time to help others without pay
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Giving your time to help others without pay is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Giving your time to help others without pay.
